ddls:
CREATE TABLE "cities" (
  "city_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each city */,
  "city_name" TEXT /* Name of the city */,
  "country" TEXT /* Country where the city is located */,
  "latitude" REAL /* Latitude of the city */,
  "longitude" REAL /* Longitude of the city */,
  "population" INTEGER /* Population of the city */,
  "timezone" TEXT /* Timezone of the city */,
  "elevation" REAL /* Elevation of the city in meters */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("city_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "weather_conditions" (
  "city_id" INTEGER /* ID of the city */,
  "max_temp" REAL /* Maximum temperature in Celsius */,
  "min_temp" REAL /* Minimum temperature in Celsius */,
  "humidity" INTEGER /* Humidity percentage */,
  "cloudiness" INTEGER /* Cloudiness percentage */,
  "wind_speed" REAL /* Wind speed in km/h */,
  "pressure" REAL /* Atmospheric pressure in hPa */,
  "visibility" REAL /* Visibility in kilometers */,
  "description" TEXT /* Current weather description */,
  "unit_id" INTEGER /* ID of the unit of measurement */,
  "recorded_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the data was recorded */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("city_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_weather_conditions_unit_id FOREIGN KEY ("unit_id") REFERENCES units ("unit_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "weather_forecasts" (
  "forecast_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each forecast */,
  "city_id" INTEGER /* ID of the city */,
  "forecast_date" TEXT /* Date of the forecast */,
  "temperature" REAL /* Forecasted temperature in Celsius */,
  "min_temp" REAL /* Forecasted minimum temperature in Celsius */,
  "humidity" INTEGER /* Forecasted humidity percentage */,
  "cloudiness" INTEGER /* Forecasted cloudiness percentage */,
  "wind_speed" REAL /* Forecasted wind speed in km/h */,
  "pressure" REAL /* Forecasted atmospheric pressure in hPa */,
  "visibility" REAL /* Forecasted visibility in kilometers */,
  "description" TEXT /* Forecasted weather description */,
  "unit_id" INTEGER /* ID of the unit of measurement */,
  "forecast_source" TEXT /* Source of the forecast (e.g., model name) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("forecast_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_weather_forecasts_city_id FOREIGN KEY ("city_id") REFERENCES cities ("city_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_weather_forecasts_unit_id FOREIGN KEY ("unit_id") REFERENCES units ("unit_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "units" (
  "unit_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each unit */,
  "unit_name" TEXT /* Name of the unit */,
  "unit_symbol" TEXT /* Symbol of the unit */,
  "unit_type" TEXT /* Type of the unit (e.g., temperature, speed) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("unit_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., meteorologist, researcher, admin) */,
  "password_hash" TEXT /* Hashed password for authentication */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of user creation */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last login */,
  "team_id" INTEGER /* ID of the team the user belongs to */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)
column_names: Country where the city is located
Elevation of the city in meters
ID of the unit of measurement
Timestamp when the data was recorded
ID of the unit of measurement
Source of the forecast (e.g., model name)
Type of the unit (e.g., temperature, speed)
Role of the user (e.g., meteorologist, researcher, admin)
ID of the team the user belongs to
